steps.title: Find the context clue
steps.content: The blank describes the Wi-Fi alternative compared to radar and sonar, which are "too costly for consumer use." The very next clause explains that Wi-Fi changes "can give users a cheap way" to detect people through walls. The blank must echo that cost contrast.

steps.title: Test each choice against the clue
steps.content:
• Choice A — unmistakable: Unmistakable means easy to recognize or obvious. The passage contrasts cost, not clarity or recognizability. Violates the precision rule — the word must fit the specific context, not just sound plausible. ✗
  • Choice B — inexpensive: Inexpensive directly echoes "cheap way" and contrasts with "too costly." A comparatively inexpensive alternative is exactly what the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice C — infrequent: Infrequent means rare or seldom occurring. The passage never addresses how often the Wi-Fi method is used; frequency is not the point of the contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — impractical: Impractical means not workable. The passage says the Wi-Fi method can count people and identify gestures, directly contradicting the idea that it is impractical. ✗
That matches Choice B — inexpensive.
